title: The Handsome Guy is First in Line / Yaoi Girls from OVERSEAS!!!
overview: Meet the enthusiastic customers of the bookstore who bring excitement and stimulation to Honda-san’s job.

title: Let Me Introduce My Crazy Colleagues in This Bookstore! / Books, Written Instructions, and Me / Go! Azarashi-san
overview: The long holiday is coming up, and the bookstore is swamped by a ton of shipments. How do the staff make it through?!

title: A Battle Without Honor or Humanity / Lost in Manga
overview: Honda-san gives us insight into the interactions between a bookstore and a publisher.

title: A Reception Training of Hell / Mission: An Outside Work-ish Job
overview: It’s Honda-san’s turn to attend a customer service coaching session.

title: An INQUIRY / It's a Book Signing!! Everyone Gather Up / A Fascinating Erotic Story
overview: Honda is faced with restocking popular titles, dealing with energetic foreigners, overseeing the visit of a famous author, and mulling over questions of censorship.

title: The Man with a Secret Power / Our Fair-Day War
overview: Honda-san introduces Assistant Manager Majutsushi, who has the uncanny ability of knowing exactly which books to order.

title: Tell Me! Wholesaler-san / A Networking Drinking Party for Booksellers
overview: Honda-san teaches us about the wonderful people known as wholesalers.

title: Bookstores are so Wonderful! / The Shelves with and without Restrictions / Go for it, Azarashi-san ~The Hidden Ambition~
overview: The bookstore is visited by all kinds of customers, some of whom like books, and some of whom love bookstores.

title: A Book about Life, Death, and Rebirth
overview: Books go out of print, but under certain special circumstances, they can be brought back from the dead.

title: An Outstanding Bookseller / Swapping Section Heads
overview: What makes a good bookseller? The answer may not be as straightforward as it seems.

title: The Alternate Skull-face Bookseller Honda-san / You Can Quit Your Job Whenever You Want
overview: If you’ve ever worked in retail, you’ve dealt with nasty customers. But what if the good customers are worse than the bad ones?

title: Merry Christmas, Mr. Bookstore / It's Time to Close
overview: Christmas is a time of madness at the bookstore. Closing time is also a time of madness at the bookstore.
